feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Requirement for GRN in Different Plant While Invoice Booking Remains in Billing Plant
entry.content:
We have two plants in SAP:

  • Plant 1200 – Billing rights are available only in this plant. Vendor invoices can be booked only against this plant.
  • Plant 1300 – Material is physically delivered and consumed in this plant.

Current process:

  1. We create the PO in Plant 1200 because invoice booking is allowed only there and delivery address is selected as 1300.
  2. The material is physically delivered to Plant 1300.
  3. During GRN, the receipt has to be posted in Plant 1200, although the material is actually received in Plant 1300.
  4. Plant 1200 has Quality Management (QM) activated, so GRN places the material into quality inspection stock and requires QA clearance before usage.

Requirement / Clarification Needed:

Please advise on the best SAP solution for the following scenario:

  1. Is it possible to selectively bypass or deactivate QM inspection for specific materials in Plant 1200 while retaining QM for other materials?

OR

  1. Is there a standard SAP process whereby:
    • PO and invoice booking remain in Plant 1200,
    • Physical receipt and GRN can be posted directly in Plant 1300,
    • While maintaining proper accounting and inventory tracking?

entry.title: Zertifizierung zur FI-Beraterin für die Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hallo zusammen,

Die Module S4F10, S4F12, S4F13, S4F15 und S4F17 habe ich bereits absolviert. Derzeit bin ich als S/4HANA Key Userin im Bereich Finance tätig, aktuell auf der S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.

Mich interessiert die Zertifizierung zur FI-Beraterin für die Cloud Edition. Handelt es sich dabei um die Zertifizierung C_TS4FI?

Wenn ich es richtig verstanden habe, wäre dafür ein Abonnement des SAP Learning Hub empfehlenswert. 

Folgende Informationen habe ich auf der SAP-Internetseite gefunden:

„ Welche Kosten entstehen für den Erwerb und die Sicherstellung der Zertifizierung?

Um Ihre SAP-Zertifizierung zu erhalten und aufrechtzuerhalten:  Mit einem Abonnement des SAP Learning Hub erhalten Sie Zugang zu Live-Schulungen mit Experten, On-Demand-Übungssystemen, erweiterten Analysen, vier Prüfungsversuchen und kurzen, unbeaufsichtigten Assessments. Diese exklusive Lösung bietet Ihnen alles, was Sie zur Vorbereitung, zum Bestehen und zur Sicherstellung Ihrer SAP-Zertifizierungen benötigen – mit nur einem Abonnement. Die nächsten Abonnementkosten betragen pro Nutzer. Erhältlich direkt im SAP Store oder über Ihren SAP-Ansprechpartner. SAP PartnerEdge-Mitglieder erfahren mehr über den SAP Learning Hub (Partner Edition) und können ihn abonnieren . "        

Verstehe ich es richtig, dass man für 1.488,00 USD Zugang zum Learning Hub sowie vier Prüfungsversuche für die Zertifizierung erhält?

Vielen Dank für Ihre Unterstützung.

Liebe Grüße

Laura

entry.title: Asset Revaluation in S/4HANA Public Cloud – Transfer from Clearing Account to Equity
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm working with Asset Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for Portugal and have a question about the standard revaluation process.

In the Post Asset Revaluation app, the credit side is posted to a clearing account defined in the standard account determination in CBC. I don't think it makes sense to change this configuration to post directly to the final equity account required by local accounting, as these account determinations appear to be part of the fit-to-standard setup.

For example, for different Portuguese asset classes, SAP delivers different offsetting accounts, such as:

  • S350431 – Intangible Assets
  • KTAUFG – Offsetting Account for Revaluation
  • 99035195 – IA - Goodwill Offset (PT)

Since the offsetting account varies by asset class, I assume this configuration is intentional and should not be changed.

The reason I'm asking is that, after posting the revaluation, if I check the Financial Statement – Balance Sheet app, the equity account where I would expect the revaluation reserve to be reflected still has a zero balance, while the amount remains in the clearing account.

My questions are:

  • Is the balance automatically transferred from the clearing account to the final equity account, or does this require a manual journal entry?
  • If it is automatic, how and when does this happen, and in which app or process?
  • If it is manual, is this the recommended approach in Public Cloud, and does it affect the link between the journal entry and the fixed asset?

My assumption is that, because SAP delivers different offsetting accounts for each asset class, this transfer is probably not automatic. However, I'd like to understand the standard SAP approach and hear from anyone who has implemented this process.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: The role template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT contains the deprecated catalog SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_IMG
entry.content:
During an audit, the invalid catalog SAP_CORE_BC_BCT_IMG was identified. How can this catalog be completely removed?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Line item numbering in RK sales orders
entry.content:
Hi sales experts,

When creating a RK invoice correction, the credit lines are created first and then the debit lines. The numbering is 10, 20, 30, etc.. In our current ECC system, the first line is the credit, the 2nd the debit and so on. The numbering is 10, 11, 20, 21 and so on. Is it possible in s/4hana public cloud to have the same numbering? This makes the invoice correction much easier to read for the customer.

many thanks,

Sanne

entry.title: Set up Authorization for Ledger Group not working
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

I´m having trouble setting up authorization for ex. when using App Post Journal Entries when posting ledger specific.

Requirement: If a user is using a specific ledger group, they should not have the authorisation.

We have two types of user:
Users – who are authorised to make ledger-specific postings for ledgers 0L (IFRS) and 2L (HGB)
Users – who are authorised to make ledger-specific postings for ledger S1 (Tax)

If no ledger group is specified, the posting will be made to all ledgers.

Settings were configured for these roles in the system (see screenshot below), as the object ‘Company code/Ledger/Record type/Version’ (BUKRS_GLRLDNR_GLRRCTY_GLRVERS) was available in all of these roles.

for all the roles ending with TAX_S1 settings are the same, as in this screenshot:

It works correctly with Ledgergroup S1.

Unfortunately, it doesn´t work for 0L,2L.

settings are the same when role ends with "HGB_IFRS"

How can that be? What else can I check?

 

Thanks

Best,

Carla

entry.title: Periodic billing for a service material
entry.content:
Hello Community,

Do you know how to change the billing plan type from 90 to 92 (periodic billing) in a sales order for a service material. I want to be able to work with billing rule 6 (periodic billing rent)

I don t want to use service contracts

entry.title: Output Parameter Determination: extend the condition parameters to include standard fields
entry.content:
Hi Anyone,

This specific example relates to the “Handling Unit” business rule. The goal is to be able to print material-specific HU labels. The material/product ID should be used for this purpose. Product: SAP Public Cloud!

The standard field “Material” is not available in the table column selection:
The result list contains 39 parameters but material is not available. Is there a way to extend the condition parameters to include standard fields?

 

What we’ve already tried—extending with a custom field:
We created a custom field named “YY1_MATERIAL” and copied the standard field using a BADI. Unfortunately, the only BADI is executed too late and only allows changes at the time the form is output.

Best regards

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud in a construction or contracting company
entry.content:
Has anyone here implemented SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud in a construction or contracting company? I'm currently evaluating SAP Public Cloud for a contractor and would appreciate the opportunity to connect with someone who has real implementation experience, particularly around project management, subcontracting, procurement, and cost control.

Looking specifically for construction/EPC contractors in Saudi Arabia, UAE, or Egypt that have implemented SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (GROW with SAP or Public Edition).
Thanks!

entry.title: Customer and bank reference number in MT940 and excel
entry.content:
Request for updating of Bank reference and Customer Reference in SAP Clearing Document

Even though our MT940 and Excel statement file contains both Bank Reference and Customer references details, however these are not capturing in the accounting document while generating the document clearing entries.

Our requirement is to have both reference IDs must be populated across all 3 GL Accounts - Main bank GL A/c, Payment clearing GL A/c and Receipt clearing GL A/c).

 We don’t find the Configuration / Implementation nodes for the same

Please help us to fix the same or suggest how do we handle this requirement

entry.title: Adding custom field in "Receive Products" app
entry.content:
Is there a way we can add new custom field or custom button in "Receive Products" app in SAP S/4 Hana public cloud? 

I have checked from in-app extensibility, but there is no "Add field" on that app. 

So, is there any other way, using like "Build application studio" or "Eclipse"?

entry.title: Enable edit access for field selection under Account Assignment Category.
entry.content:
We have observed that the Project field is not visible at the Purchase Requisition (PR) level when using Account Assignment Category "F".

Upon reviewing the configuration under Maintain Account Assignment Categories, we found that the Project field is currently set as Hidden by default. To meet the business requirement, we need to change the field status from Hidden to Optional Entry so that users can enter project-related information during PR creation.

However, despite accessing the configuration in edit mode, the system is not allowing us to modify the field settings. We request your assistance in identifying the cause of this restriction and enabling the necessary changes at the earliest so that the business requirement can be fulfilled.

entry.title: Service Entries substitution
entry.content:
unfortunately we notice more friction with our Service Entry workflows than anticipated. partly system related, party behavior related.

we have scenario of cost controller assigned to WO's as resp person (cost controller) and with that receiving the SE in his inbox for review/commenting/rejecting before SE is forwarded to Manager for approval. we notice though a couple of times now SE's return to cost controller without any clear reason why. 

3 questions:

1. what exactly happens with workflow items still in the inbox of a person at the moment he activates substitution. we have the suspicion there were still items in manager's inbox at the moment substitution was switched on and that items were returned from that moment

2. do forwarded items also appear in substitutor's inbox ?

3. is our setup of Cost controller no access to SE approve but having a role in review SE's but using the forward approach OK with SAP best practices? we deliberately did not introduce a 2 step approval approach to avoid additional friction / bureaucracy for the rest of the company

entry.title: Cash Flow Statement (Direct Method) - Problem
entry.content:
The Cash Flow Statement (Direct Method) is not reflecting supplier payment transactions, even though the postings exist in the system.

Supplier payments have been correctly created using the Post Outgoing Payments app, and the corresponding accounting documents are posted successfully. However, these transactions are not being displayed in the Cash Flow report, and the report values remain at zero.

All relevant configuration has been checked, including G/L account assignment and reconciliation account mapping. The issue persists even after adjustments to the account setup.

Please find attached a screenshot showing that the Cash Flow Statement remains at zero despite existing accounting entries.

entry.title: Cash Flow Statement (Direct Method) - Problem
entry.content:
The Cash Flow Statement (Direct Method) is not reflecting supplier payment transactions, even though the postings exist in the system.

Supplier payments have been correctly created using the Post Outgoing Payments app, and the corresponding accounting documents are posted successfully. However, these transactions are not being displayed in the Cash Flow report, and the report values remain at zero.

All relevant configuration has been checked, including G/L account assignment and reconciliation account mapping. The issue persists even after adjustments to the account setup.

Please find attached a screenshot showing that the Cash Flow Statement remains at zero despite existing accounting entries.

entry.title: #COE#RFL01- Change company code currency from EUR to VND
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

When creating our company code the VND company currency was not available in SAP Cloud Public 2 SL.

So we created Company XX , that was set up with the € as its company currency.

We decided to use VND as the transaction currency instead of the company currency, but entries in euro currency like balance carryforward, are being generated, which makes it difficult to use the transaction currency.

Since SAP now supports the VND currency., we're wondering if SAP offers a solution for customers who had to find workarounds because the VND currency wasn't available in the system then?

Do you know if there's a way to switch to the VND currency, or do we have no choice but to create a new company in VND?

If you’ve encountered this type of situation, please feel free to share your advice, tips, and solutions.

Thank you all !

entry.title: #COE#RFL01- Change company code currency from EUR to VND
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

When creating our company code the VND company currency was not available in SAP Cloud Public 2 SL.

So we created Company XX , that was set up with the € as its company currency.

We decided to use VND as the transaction currency instead of the company currency, but entries in euro currency like balance carryforward, are being generated, which makes it difficult to use the transaction currency.

Since SAP now supports the VND  currency., we're wondering if SAP offers a solution for customers who had to find workarounds because the VND currency wasn't available in the system then?

Do you know if there's a way to switch to the VND currency, or do we have no choice but to create a new company in VND?

If you’ve encountered this type of situation, please feel free to share your advice, tips, and solutions.

Thank you all !

entry.title: Invoice payment block using QM control key for Embedded EWM Goods Receipt in Public Cloud 2602
entry.content:
Even after maintaining QM Control key '0007' for invoice payment block in QM view, the system is not restricting the payment of GR through WM inbound delivery in Public cloud . The purchase order QA control key shows: 'supplier release, invoice block'.

Inspection lots are created with origin '17' with inspection type 01 maintained. Quality info record also maintained.

entry.title: Error in Manage Automatic Payments app
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team

In Manage Automatic Payments app, In Payments Processed no exists any icon to add documents.

Are there any places to add documents in Manage Automatic Payments app?

Thanks

entry.title: Unable to assign new GL account to custom movement type which is a copy of existing movement type
entry.content:
We have a business scenario where separate consumption account need to be determined if the material is issued to cost center for R&D purposes and regular production consumption. 

We created a custom movement type 901 which is a copy of movement type 201 and assign a account modifier ZBR. Please see attached screenshot for additional information.

while setting up automatic account determination, we do not see general modification "ZBR" in the drop down. Please see screenshot for additional information. 

We cannot create a separate valuation class as it means we have to create a separate material for the same material which is practically infeasible suggestion.

Is there any enhancement option available to change the account assignment or is this item on the roadmap?
